### 776. `POST` `/api/v1/webhooks/notifications/register`

**Summary:** Register a webhook URL

**Tags:** Webhook Notifications

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Register a new webhook URL with an event filter. Returns the webhook ID and signing secret.

**Request Body:** `RegisterWebhookRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  | Organization identifier |
| `url` | str | Yes |  | Target HTTPS URL |
| `events` | List | Yes |  | Event types to subscribe to |
| `description` | Optional | No | None |  |

### 781. `POST` `/api/v1/webhooks/verify/`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/webhooks/verify/

**Tags:** webhook-verifier

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Auto-detect the webhook provider from request headers and verify the signature.  Reads the raw
request body and all headers.  The ``X-Webhook-Secret-<Provider>`` header (e.g. ``X-Webhook-Secret-
Github``) is used to pass the shared secret without exposing it in the JSON body.  Alternatively,
callers can supply secrets via the ``X-Webhook-Secrets`` JSON header: ``{"github": "s3cr3t"}``.
Returns a VerificationResult JSON object.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 782. `GET` `/api/v1/webhooks/verify/stats`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/webhooks/verify/stats

**Tags:** webhook-verifier

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return webhook verification pass/fail rates per provider for the current org.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 783. `POST` `/api/v1/webhooks/verify/detect`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/webhooks/verify/detect

**Tags:** webhook-verifier

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Detect the webhook provider from a set of HTTP headers (dry-run, no verification).  Returns
``{"provider": "<name>"}`` or ``{"provider": null}`` if unrecognised.

**Request Body:** `DetectRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `headers` | Dict | Yes |  | HTTP headers from the incoming webhook |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 784. `POST` `/api/v1/webhooks/verify/{provider}`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/webhooks/verify/{provider}

**Tags:** webhook-verifier

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Verify a webhook against a specific named provider.  ``provider`` must be one of: github, gitlab,
jira, servicenow, slack, pagerduty, stripe, custom.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `provider` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Request Body:** `VerifyRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `payload` | str | Yes |  | Raw webhook payload (UTF-8 string or hex-encoded bytes) |
| `signature` | str | Yes |  | Signature header value sent by the provider |
| `secret` | str | Yes |  | Shared secret configured for this integration |
| `timestamp` | Optional | No | None | Timestamp header value (required for Slack / Stripe) |
| `algorithm` | Optional | No | sha256 | HMAC algorithm for CUSTOM provider (sha256, sha1, sha512, md5) |
| `ip_address` | Optional | No | None | Source IP for audit log |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Summary:** Okta Event Hook verification challenge

**Tags:** Webhooks

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Okta sends a GET with x-okta-verification-challenge header during hook registration. This endpoint
echoes the challenge value back so Okta can confirm ownership.

|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|

pending outbox items that have either never been attempted or whose next_retry_at time has passed.

### 810. `POST` `/api/v1/webhooks/jira`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/webhooks/jira

**Tags:** webhooks-receivers

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Receive webhook events from Jira for bidirectional sync.  Note: This endpoint uses synchronous def
(not async def) because it performs blocking SQLite operations. FastAPI automatically runs sync
endpoints in a threadpool to avoid blocking the event loop.

**Request Body:** `JiraWebhookPayload`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `webhookEvent` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `issue` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `changelog` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `user` | Optional | No | None |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 814. `POST` `/api/v1/webhooks/github`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/webhooks/github

**Tags:** webhooks-receivers

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Receive webhook events from GitHub for CI/CD integration.  Supported events: - **push**: Extracts
changed files → triggers scanner ingest + brain pipeline. - **pull_request** (opened/synchronize):
Queues security scan for PR diff.  Authentication: HMAC-SHA256 via ``X-Hub-Signature-256`` header
when ``FIXOPS_GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET`` is set.  Without the env var, all payloads are accepted
(development mode).

**Request Body:** `GitHubWebhookPayload`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `action` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `ref` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `before` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `after` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `repository` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `sender` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `commits` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `pull_request` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `head_commit` | Optional | No | None |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 841. `POST` `/api/v1/jira-sync/configure`

**Summary:** Configure Jira sync engine

**Tags:** jira-sync

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Set (and persist) the Jira connection configuration and sync policy.  The configuration is stored in
the sync engine's SQLite database and survives process restarts.

**Request Body:** `ConfigureRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `jira_url` | str | Yes |  | Jira base URL, e.g. https://example.atlassian.net |
| `user_email` | str | Yes |  | Jira user email for API auth |
| `api_token` | str | Yes |  | Jira API token or PAT |
| `project_key` | str | Yes |  | Jira project key, e.g. SEC |
| `default_issue_type` | str | No | Bug | Default Jira issue type |
| `sync_direction` | str | No | bidirectional | bidirectional \| finding_to_jira \| jira_to_finding |
| `conflict_resolution` | str | No | newest_wins | newest_wins \| jira_wins \| finding_wins \| manual |
| `labels` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `component_name` | Optional | No | None | Jira component name to assign |
| `webhook_secret` | Optional | No | None | Secret for validating Jira webhook calls |
| `field_mappings` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `jira_to_finding_status` | Optional | No | None | Override Jira status → finding status mapping |
| `finding_to_jira_transition` | Optional | No | None | Override finding status → Jira transition name mapping |
| `severity_to_priority` | Optional | No | None | Override severity → Jira priority mapping |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `ConfigureResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `success` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `configured` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `project_key` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `sync_direction` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `conflict_resolution` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 842. `POST` `/api/v1/jira-sync/sync-all`

**Summary:** Sync a batch of findings to Jira

**Tags:** jira-sync

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Push a list of findings to Jira, creating or updating issues as needed.  Each finding dict must
contain a ``finding_id`` or ``id`` field. Returns per-finding results and aggregate counters.

**Request Body:** `SyncAllRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `findings` | List | Yes |  | List of finding dicts, each with a finding_id or id field |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SyncAllResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `total` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `succeeded` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `failed` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `skipped` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `results` | List | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 843. `POST` `/api/v1/jira-sync/sync-finding`

**Summary:** Sync a single finding to Jira

**Tags:** jira-sync

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Create or update the Jira issue that corresponds to the given finding.  If a Jira link already
exists for this finding the issue is updated. If no link exists a new Jira issue is created and the
link is recorded.

**Request Body:** `SyncFindingRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  | Unique finding identifier |
| `finding_data` | Dict | Yes |  | Finding fields: title, severity, description, cve_id, source, etc. |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SyncResultResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `jira_issue_key` | Optional | Yes |  |  |
| `status` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `direction` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `detail` | Dict | Yes |  |  |
| `synced_at` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 844. `POST` `/api/v1/jira-sync/sync-status`

**Summary:** Propagate finding status change to Jira

**Tags:** jira-sync

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Transition the linked Jira ticket to reflect the finding's new status.  Requires a pre-existing link
between ``finding_id`` and a Jira issue (established via ``sync-finding``). Uses the configured
``finding_to_jira_transition`` mapping to determine the Jira transition name.

**Request Body:** `SyncStatusRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  | Finding to update |
| `new_status` | str | Yes |  | New finding status, e.g. resolved, closed, in_progress |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SyncResultResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `jira_issue_key` | Optional | Yes |  |  |
| `status` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `direction` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `detail` | Dict | Yes |  |  |
| `synced_at` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 857. `POST` `/api/v1/slack/commands`

**Summary:** Slack slash command handler

**Tags:** Slack

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Handle a Slack slash command (e.g. /status, /findings, /sla).  Slack sends form-encoded data; this
endpoint decodes it, optionally verifies the HMAC signature, and returns a BlockKit response.

**Request Body:** `str`

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SlackCommandResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `response_type` | str | No | ephemeral | 'in_channel' or 'ephemeral' |
| `blocks` | list | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `text` | Optional | No | None |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Tags:** servicenow

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Trigger a CMDB CI sync from ServiceNow.  Pulls Configuration Items for each specified CI class and
upserts them as ALDECI assets in the sync state database.

**Request Body:** `CMDBSyncRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `connection_id` | str | Yes |  | Connection ID to sync from |
| `ci_classes` | List | No | ['cmdb_ci_server'] | CI class names to pull |
| `query` | str | No | operational_status=1 | ServiceNow encoded query filter |
| `limit` | int | No | 100 | Max CIs per class |

**Responses:**


**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 873. `POST` `/api/v1/servicenow/sync/incidents`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/servicenow/sync/incidents

**Tags:** servicenow

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Push ALDECI alerts as ServiceNow incidents.  Transforms each alert using severity-to-urgency mapping
and creates incidents via the ServiceNow Table API. Records the sys_id mapping for bidirectional
tracking.

**Request Body:** `IncidentPushRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|

| `metric_type` | str | No | value | Metric type |

### 941. `GET` `/api/v1/unified-dashboard/ciso`

**Summary:** CISO Executive Dashboard

**Tags:** unified-dashboard

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Returns all CISO KPIs in a single call: posture score, SLA compliance, critical findings, compliance
coverage, incidents, and threat intelligence. Results cached for 60 seconds.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | str | No | Organisation identifier |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `DashboardLayout`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | str | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `widgets` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `owner` | str | No | system |  |

### 949. `GET` `/api/v1/metrics/metric/{name}`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/metrics/metric/{name}

**Tags:** metrics-aggregator

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Return a single named metric from the latest snapshot.  Raises 404 if the metric is not found.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `name` | path | str | Yes | — |
| `org_id` | query | str | No | Organisation identifier |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Metric`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Metric identifier |
| `value` | float | Yes |  | Numeric metric value |
| `unit` | str | No |  | Unit label (e.g. 'score', 'count', '%') |
| `category` | MetricCategory | Yes |  | Metric category |
| `trend` | MetricTrend | No | MetricTrend.STABLE | Trend direction |
| `change_pct` | float | No | 0.0 | Percentage change vs previous period |
| `period` | str | No | current | Period label |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |

### 966. `PUT` `/api/v1/rate-limits/config`

**Summary:** Update endpoint tier mapping or per-key rate limit override

**Tags:** rate-limits

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Update endpoint tier mapping and/or per-key request limit.  - Provide ``path_pattern`` + ``tier`` to
register a new endpoint mapping. - Provide ``api_key_id`` + ``requests_per_minute`` to set a per-key
override   (set ``requests_per_minute`` to 0 to remove the override).

**Request Body:** `EndpointConfigUpdate`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `path_pattern` | Optional | No | None | Regex pattern for the endpoint path (e.g. '^/api/v1/custom'). |
| `tier` | Optional | No | None | Rate limit tier to assign to the path pattern. |
| `api_key_id` | Optional | No | None | API key ID to apply a per-key request-per-minute override. |
| `requests_per_minute` | Optional | No | None | Requests per minute for the per-key override. 0 removes override. |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 996. `POST` `/api/v1/sla-management/policies`

**Summary:** Create or update a scoped SLA policy

**Tags:** SLA Management

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Create or replace an SLA policy scoped to org / team / asset-tier.  - If no team_id/asset_tier: org-
wide default policy. - If team_id given: applies only to that team. - If asset_tier given: applies
only to that tier. - Most-specific scope wins at assignment time.

**Request Body:** `CreatePolicyRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Human-readable policy name |
| `team_id` | Optional | No | None | Scope to a specific team |
| `asset_tier` | Optional | No | None | Scope to asset tier: tier1–tier5 |
| `severity_deadlines` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined | SLA deadline in hours per severity level |
| `framework_overrides` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined | Per-framework deadline overrides (pci-dss, hipaa, soc2, etc.) |
| `business_hours_only` | bool | No | False | Count only business hours (Mon–Fri 09:00–17:00) against SLA |
| `tz_name` | str | No | UTC | Timezone for business hours calculation |
| `escalation_rules` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Escalation contacts per severity |
| `enabled` | bool | No | True |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SLAPolicyV2`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | str | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `team_id` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `asset_tier` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `severity_deadlines` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `framework_overrides` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `business_hours_only` | bool | No | False |  |
| `tz_name` | str | No | UTC |  |
| `escalation_rules` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `enabled` | bool | No | True |  |
| `created_at` | datetime | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `updated_at` | datetime | No | PydanticUndefined |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 998. `POST` `/api/v1/sla-management/assign`

**Summary:** Auto-assign SLA to a finding

**Tags:** SLA Management

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Assign an SLA deadline to a finding.  Deadline is computed from: 1. Applicable SLA policy (org →
team → tier scope) 2. Active compliance frameworks (strictest wins) 3. Asset tier multiplier (tier1
= 0.5x, tier5 = 2x) 4. Business-hours-only mode (if policy enables it)

**Request Body:** `AssignSLARequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `severity` | str | Yes |  | critical \| high \| medium \| low |
| `discovered_at` | Optional | No | None | Discovery timestamp (UTC); defaults to now |
| `team_id` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `asset_tier` | str | No | tier3 | tier1–tier5 |
| `frameworks` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Active compliance frameworks (e.g. pci-dss, hipaa) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SLAAssignment`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | str | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `team_id` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `asset_tier` | str | No | tier3 |  |
| `severity` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `frameworks` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `discovered_at` | datetime | Yes |  |  |
| `deadline` | datetime | Yes |  |  |
| `business_hours` | bool | No | False |  |
| `status` | SLAStatusV2 | No | SLAStatusV2.WITHIN_SLA |  |
| `pct_elapsed` | float | No | 0.0 |  |
| `escalation_level` | EscalationLevel | No | EscalationLevel.NONE |  |
| `breached_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `resolved_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `created_at` | datetime | No | PydanticUndefined |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 999. `GET` `/api/v1/sla-management/status/{finding_id}`

**Summary:** Get real-time SLA status for a finding

**Tags:** SLA Management

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Compute and return current SLA status.  Status values: - ``within_sla``       — deadline not yet
reached - ``approaching``      — ≥80% of time elapsed - ``breached``         — deadline passed -
``severely_breached`` — 2× deadline elapsed - ``resolved``         — finding was remediated -
``exempt``           — exception approved / risk accepted

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `finding_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SLAStatusResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `status` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `severity` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `asset_tier` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `deadline` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `discovered_at` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `pct_elapsed` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `escalation_level` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `breached_at` | Optional | Yes |  |  |
| `resolved_at` | Optional | Yes |  |  |
| `frameworks` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `business_hours` | bool | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1000. `POST` `/api/v1/sla-management/exceptions`

**Summary:** Request an SLA exception

**Tags:** SLA Management

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Submit an SLA exception request.  Types: - ``risk_acceptance``      — accept the risk with
justification + expiry - ``extended_deadline``    — request more time (supply new_deadline) -
``false_positive``       — mark finding as false positive (with evidence) - ``compensating_control``
— document a compensating control

**Request Body:** `ExceptionRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `exception_type` | ExceptionType | Yes |  |  |
| `justification` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `requested_by` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `expiry_date` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `evidence` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `new_deadline` | Optional | No | None | Required for extended_deadline exceptions |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SLAException`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | str | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `exception_type` | ExceptionType | Yes |  |  |
| `justification` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `requested_by` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `approved_by` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `status` | ExceptionStatus | No | ExceptionStatus.PENDING |  |
| `expiry_date` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `evidence` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `new_deadline` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `created_at` | datetime | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `updated_at` | datetime | No | PydanticUndefined |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1008. `GET` `/api/v1/events/stream`

**Summary:** Resumable SSE security event stream

**Tags:** SSE Events

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Stream security events as Server-Sent Events (SSE).  CrowdStrike-parity resumable stream for SIEM
integration.  **Resuming after disconnect** — include the ``Last-Event-ID`` header with the last
received event ID.  The server replays all missed events from that point before switching to live
delivery.  **Keep-alive** — a SSE comment (`: keepalive`) is sent every 15 s so proxies and load
balancers do not close idle connections.  **Filtering** — pass ``?event_type=alert`` to receive only
alerts.  **Example curl**: ``` curl -N -H "X-API-Key: $KEY" \
"https://host/api/v1/events/stream?org_id=acme&event_type=alert" ```

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | str | Yes | Organization ID |
| `event_type` | query | Optional | No | Filter by event type: alert \| finding \| incident \| anomaly \| audit |

**Responses:**


**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1009. `POST` `/api/v1/oauth2/token`

**Summary:** Issue OAuth2 client credentials JWT

**Tags:** OAuth2

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

RFC 6749 §4.4 Client Credentials Grant. Supply `client_id` (APIKey.id) and `client_secret` (raw API
key) as application/x-www-form-urlencoded. Returns a 30-minute Bearer JWT accepted by all ALDECI
endpoints.

**Request Body:** `str`

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `TokenResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `access_token` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `token_type` | str | No | bearer |  |
| `expires_in` | int | No | 1800 |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/backups

**Description:**

Create a new backup snapshot.

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `backup_type` | BackupType | No | BackupType.FULL |  |
| `databases` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `encrypt` | bool | No | False |  |
| `retention_days` | int | No | 30 |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `BackupRecord`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `type` | BackupType | Yes |  |  |
| `status` | BackupStatus | Yes |  |  |
| `databases` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `file_path` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `file_size_bytes` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `checksum` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `encrypted` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `created_at` | datetime | Yes |  |  |
| `completed_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `retention_days` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1042. `POST` `/api/v1/changelog/generate`

**Summary:** Generate changelog from commits

**Tags:** changelog

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Parse the provided commit log text and return a formatted changelog.  Accepts conventional commits
(``feat:``, ``fix:``, ...) and beast-mode commits (``beast-mode(type): ...``).

**Request Body:** `GenerateRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `commits` | str | Yes |  | Raw commit log text. Each line may be plain commit messages or tabular format: <sha>\t<author>\t<date>\t<message> |
| `version` | str | No | 1.0.0 | Version label for this changelog |
| `format` | OutputFormat | No | OutputFormat.MARKDOWN | Output format |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `GenerateResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `version` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `format` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `content` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `entry_count` | int | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1043. `GET` `/api/v1/changelog/unreleased`

**Summary:** Changes since last tag

**Tags:** changelog

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return all unreleased commits since the given tag, with a suggested next version based on change
types.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `since_tag` | query | str | No | Git tag to diff from (empty = all commits) |
| `current_version` | query | str | No | Current version for bump suggestion |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `UnreleasedResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `since_tag` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `entry_count` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `entries` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `suggested_version` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1044. `POST` `/api/v1/changelog/suggest-version`

**Summary:** Suggest next semver

**Tags:** changelog

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Parse commits and suggest the appropriate next semantic version.  Rules: - Breaking change → major
bump - New feature → minor bump - Bug fix / other → patch bump

**Request Body:** `SuggestVersionRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `commits` | str | Yes |  | Raw commit log text |
| `current_version` | str | No | 0.0.0 | Current semver string |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SuggestVersionResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `current_version` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `suggested_version` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `bump_type` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `entry_count` | int | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Summary:** Generate release notes

**Tags:** changelog

### 1078. `POST` `/api/v1/local-store/acquire-lock`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/local-store/acquire-lock

**Tags:** Local File Store

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Acquire the exclusive `.analyze.lock` for a repo.  Returns the owner descriptor (``owner_token``,
``pid``, ``acquired_at``). Callers MUST pass ``owner_token`` back to `/release-lock` to safely
release without clobbering another process's lock.

**Request Body:** `AcquireLockRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `repo_path` | str | Yes |  | Absolute path to repo root |
| `timeout` | float | No | 30.0 |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1079. `POST` `/api/v1/local-store/release-lock`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/local-store/release-lock

**Tags:** Local File Store

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Release a previously acquired lock.

**Request Body:** `ReleaseLockRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `repo_path` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `owner_token` | Optional | No | None | Token returned from acquire-lock |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1123. `POST` `/api/v1/data-pipeline/pipelines/{pipeline_id}/runs`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/data-pipeline/pipelines/{pipeline_id}/runs

**Tags:** Security Data Pipeline

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Record an execution run for a pipeline and update its throughput counters.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `pipeline_id` | path | str | Yes | — |
| `org_id` | query | str | No | default |

**Request Body:** `RecordRunRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `run_status` | str | Yes |  | queued \| running \| completed \| failed \| partial |
| `records_in` | int | No | 0 | Records read from source |
| `records_out` | int | No | 0 | Records successfully processed |
| `records_failed` | int | No | 0 | Records that failed processing |
| `duration_seconds` | int | No | 0 | Wall-clock duration of the run |
| `error_message` | Optional | No | None | Error detail if run failed |
| `started_at` | Optional | No | None | ISO-8601 run start time |
| `completed_at` | Optional | No | None | ISO-8601 run completion time |

**Responses:**


**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `ecosystem` | str | Yes |  | npm\|pypi\|maven |
| `package_name` | str | Yes |  | Package name (maven uses group/artifact) |
| `version` | str | Yes |  | Affected version |
| `cve_id` | str | Yes |  | CVE identifier |
| `fixed_in` | str | Yes |  | Version where fix is available |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1161. `GET` `/api/v1/council/calibration`

**Summary:** Model accuracy report (last 30 days)

**Tags:** council

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return accuracy metrics per model over the last N days.  Shows each model's prediction accuracy and
current voting weight. Weights are adjusted automatically as outcomes are fed back via /feedback.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `window_days` | query | int | No | Rolling window in days |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1162. `POST` `/api/v1/council/feedback`

**Summary:** Submit actual outcome for a verdict

**Tags:** council

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Feed the actual outcome of a finding back to calibrate model weights.  Call this after a human
analyst (or automated verification) confirms whether the council's verdict was correct. Correct
predictions increase a model's weight; incorrect predictions decrease it.

**Request Body:** `FeedbackRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `verdict_id` | str | Yes |  | verdict_id from CouncilVerdict |
| `actual_outcome` | str | Yes |  | Ground-truth label: TRUE_POSITIVE \| FALSE_POSITIVE \| NEEDS_REVIEW |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `FeedbackResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `status` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `verdict_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `actual_outcome` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1163. `GET` `/api/v1/council/recent-verdicts`

**Summary:** Last 50 council decisions with accuracy

**Tags:** council

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return the most recent council verdicts, including accuracy where known.  Each entry includes: -
verdict_id, verdict, confidence, agreement_pct - escalated flag, processing_ms, created_at -
actual_outcome (if feedback has been submitted) - accurate (True/False/None — None if no outcome
yet)

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `limit` | query | int | No | Max verdicts to return |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `List`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |

### 1188. `PUT` `/api/v1/apps/{app_id}`

**Summary:** Update app config

**Tags:** APP_ID Configuration

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Apply a partial update to an existing app config.  Top-level keys in ``updates`` are merged into the
current config. Nested dicts (e.g. ``policies``) are shallow-merged.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `app_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Request Body:** `UpdateAppRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `updates` | Dict | Yes |  | Keys to update in the config |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1195. `POST` `/api/v1/apps/{app_id}/validate`

**Summary:** Validate classification consistency

**Tags:** APP_ID Configuration

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Validate that policy classification level is appropriate for data classification.  Checks include: -
Policy level must meet minimum required by data type (PHI/PCI/PII → CUI minimum) - TOP_SECRET/SCI
data cannot have UNCLASSIFIED policies - ITAR in compliance list must have itar_controlled = true -
Air-gapped environments should not reference cloud-only scanners

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `app_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `ClassificationValidationResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `app_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `valid` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `data_classification` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `policy_classification_level` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `issues` | List | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1207. `GET` `/api/v1/findings`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/findings

**Tags:** findings

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

List findings with rich filtering and pagination.  Args:     severity: Filter by severity
(low|medium|high|critical)     status: Filter by status
(open|in_progress|remediated|suppressed|false_positive|accepted_risk)     connector: Filter by
source connector     cve_id: Filter by CVE ID     asset_id: Filter by asset ID     assigned_to:
Filter by assignee     date_from: Filter created after (ISO 8601)     date_to: Filter created before
(ISO 8601)     sort_by: Sort field     limit: Results per page (default 50, max 500)     offset:
Pagination offset  Returns:     Paginated findings list with total count

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `severity` | query | Optional | No | None |
| `status` | query | Optional | No | None |
| `connector` | query | Optional | No | None |
| `cve_id` | query | Optional | No | None |
| `asset_id` | query | Optional | No | None |
| `assigned_to` | query | Optional | No | None |
| `date_from` | query | Optional | No | None |
| `date_to` | query | Optional | No | None |
| `sort_by` | query | str | No | severity |
| `limit` | query | int | No | 50 |
| `offset` | query | int | No | 0 |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1218. `POST` `/api/v1/council/feedback`

**Summary:** Submit actual outcome for a verdict

**Tags:** council

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Feed the actual outcome of a finding back to calibrate model weights.  Call this after a human
analyst (or automated verification) confirms whether the council's verdict was correct. Correct
predictions increase a model's weight; incorrect predictions decrease it.

**Request Body:** `FeedbackRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `verdict_id` | str | Yes |  | verdict_id from CouncilVerdict |
| `actual_outcome` | str | Yes |  | Ground-truth label: TRUE_POSITIVE \| FALSE_POSITIVE \| NEEDS_REVIEW |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `FeedbackResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `status` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `verdict_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `actual_outcome` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1233. `POST` `/api/v1/fail/inject`

**Summary:** Inject a synthetic vulnerability (create drill)

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Inject a synthetic vulnerability finding into the FixOps pipeline.  This creates a FAIL Engine drill
— the synthetic finding will appear in the normal finding feed for the target component,
indistinguishable from a real finding. The clock starts ticking from injection time.  Available
scenarios: log4shell, sqli, ssrf, path_traversal, insecure_deserialization, hardcoded_credentials,
broken_auth, xss, crypto_weakness, supply_chain.

**Request Body:** `InjectRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `scenario` | str | Yes |  | Scenario ID to inject. One of: log4shell, sqli, ssrf, path_traversal, insecure_deserialization, hardcoded_credentials, broken_auth, xss, crypto_weakness, supply_chain — or a custom scenario ID. |
| `target_component` | str | Yes |  | The component / service to target with the synthetic finding |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  | Organisation identifier |
| `notes` | str | No |  | Optional notes for this drill (not visible to the team being tested) |
| `injected_by` | Optional | No | None | Identifier of the person / system injecting the drill |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `InjectResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `drill_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `scenario_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `scenario_name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `target_component` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `status` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `severity` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `synthetic_finding_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `injected_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `expires_at` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `message` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

| `escalated` | bool | No | False | Was the finding escalated? |

### 1239. `POST` `/api/v1/fail/drills/{drill_id}/grade`

**Summary:** Grade team response to a drill

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Compute and persist the 4-dimension drill score.  Scoring dimensions: - **Detection Speed** (30%) —
How fast was the synthetic finding noticed? - **Triage Accuracy** (25%) — Was it correctly
classified as critical/real? - **Remediation Speed** (30%) — How fast was the fix applied? -
**Communication** (15%) — Was the right team notified? Escalation followed?  Overall = weighted
average of all four dimensions (0-10 scale).

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `drill_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Request Body:** `GradeRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `override_detection_minutes` | Optional | No | None | Override auto-computed detection time (minutes from injection) |
| `override_remediation_minutes` | Optional | No | None | Override auto-computed remediation time (minutes from injection) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `GradeResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `drill_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `detection_speed` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `triage_accuracy` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `remediation_speed` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `communication` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `overall` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `grade` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `detection_minutes_actual` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `detection_minutes_target` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `triage_classification_actual` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `triage_classification_expected` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `remediation_minutes_actual` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `escalated_correctly` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `team_notified` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `feedback` | List | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1240. `DELETE` `/api/v1/fail/drills/{drill_id}`

**Summary:** Cancel an active drill

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Cancel an active drill without grading it.  The drill will be marked as cancelled and removed from
the active list. Cancelled drills are excluded from readiness scoring.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `drill_id` | path | str | Yes | — |
| `cancelled_by` | query | Optional | No | Who is cancelling the drill |
| `reason` | query | str | No | Reason for cancellation |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1241. `GET` `/api/v1/fail/neglect-zones`

**Summary:** Components with no recent security activity

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return all components that have had no security activity (scan, review, drill) within the threshold
period.  Risk amplification rules: - Component inactive 90+ days → flagged - Component inactive +
holds critical data → **urgent** - Each neglect zone includes a suggested drill scenario  Use this
to proactively target under-tested components for FAIL drills.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `threshold_days` | query | int | No | Days of inactivity to flag as neglected |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1242. `GET` `/api/v1/fail/readiness`

**Summary:** Organisation readiness score (alias)

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Compute the organisation's security readiness score based on drill history.  Readiness = rolling
average of the last 10 graded drill scores.  Returns: - Overall score (0-10) - Per-dimension
averages (detection, triage, remediation, communication) - Per-team breakdown - Trend (improving /
declining / stable) - Industry benchmark comparison - Percentile ranking

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Summary:** Organisation readiness score

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Compute the organisation's security readiness score based on drill history.  Readiness = rolling
average of the last 10 graded drill scores.  Returns: - Overall score (0-10) - Per-dimension
averages (detection, triage, remediation, communication) - Per-team breakdown - Trend (improving /
declining / stable) - Industry benchmark comparison - Percentile ranking

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1244. `GET` `/api/v1/fail/comparison`

**Summary:** Industry benchmark comparison

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Compare organisation readiness score against the industry benchmark.  The default benchmark is
6.5/10 (configurable at engine init). Returns a delta, percentile estimate, and an assessment
string.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1245. `GET` `/api/v1/fail/scenarios`

**Summary:** List available injection scenarios

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

List all available FAIL injection scenarios (built-in and custom).  Each scenario includes: -
Synthetic finding payload (CVE, CVSS, evidence) - MITRE ATT&CK technique/tactic mapping - CWE
identifiers - Expected detection timeline and triage classification - Recommended remediation
approach

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1246. `POST` `/api/v1/fail/scenarios`

**Summary:** Create a custom injection scenario

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Create a custom FAIL injection scenario.  Custom scenarios allow organisations to test detection of
domain-specific vulnerabilities that are not covered by the 10 built-in scenarios.  The
synthetic_finding payload should mimic what a real scanner would report for this vulnerability class
— the closer to reality, the more valid the test.

**Request Body:** `CreateScenarioRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `scenario_id` | str | Yes |  | Unique identifier for the scenario (snake_case) |
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Human-readable scenario name |
| `description` | str | Yes |  | Scenario description |
| `severity` | str | No | high | Severity level: critical, high, medium, low, info |
| `synthetic_finding` | Dict | Yes |  | The synthetic finding payload to inject |
| `cwe_ids` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | CWE identifiers |
| `mitre_techniques` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs |
| `mitre_tactics` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | MITRE ATT&CK tactics |
| `expected_detection_minutes` | int | No | 60 | Target detection time in minutes |
| `expected_triage_classification` | str | No | real_high | Expected triage classification for scoring |
| `expected_remediation_approach` | str | No |  | Guidance on the expected fix |
| `cvss_score` | float | No | 7.0 | CVSS base score |
| `cve_id` | Optional | No | None | Associated CVE identifier |
| `tags` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Searchable tags |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1247. `GET` `/api/v1/fail/training-data`

**Summary:** Export labeled training samples

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Export labeled training samples generated from completed drills.  Each sample includes two labeled
signals for ML feedback loops: - **Detection signal**: `detection_label` ∈ {fast, slow, very_slow,
missed} - **Triage signal**: `triage_label` ∈ {correct, incorrect, skipped}  These samples feed into
the self-learning detection and triage loops: - Loop 1: Detection model — learns what "fast
detection" looks like per scenario - Loop 2: Triage model — learns correct severity classification
per finding type

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `scenario_id` | query | Optional | No | Filter by scenario |
| `limit` | query | int | No | Maximum samples to return |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1248. `POST` `/api/v1/fail/activity`

**Summary:** Log a security activity for neglect zone tracking

**Tags:** fail-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Log a security activity event for a component.  This is used to track when components have been
scanned, reviewed, or drilled, so the neglect zone detector can accurately identify blind spots.
Activity types: scan, review, drill, pentest, audit

**Request Body:** `LogActivityRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  | Organisation identifier |
| `component` | str | Yes |  | Component / service name |
| `activity_type` | str | Yes |  | Type of activity: scan, review, drill, pentest, audit |
| `description` | str | No |  | Activity description |
| `actor` | Optional | No | None | Who performed the activity |
| `has_critical_data` | bool | No | False | Does this component hold critical data? |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1265. `POST` `/api/v1/apps/{app_id}/validate`

**Summary:** Validate classification consistency

**Tags:** APP_ID Configuration

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Validate that policy classification level is appropriate for data classification.  Checks include: -
Policy level must meet minimum required by data type (PHI/PCI/PII → CUI minimum) - TOP_SECRET/SCI
data cannot have UNCLASSIFIED policies - ITAR in compliance list must have itar_controlled = true -
Air-gapped environments should not reference cloud-only scanners

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `app_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `ClassificationValidationResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `app_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `valid` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `data_classification` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `policy_classification_level` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `issues` | List | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1274. `POST` `/api/v1/scanner-ingest/upload`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/scanner-ingest/upload

**Tags:** scanner-ingest

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Upload a scanner output file for ingestion.  Supports: ZAP, Burp, Nessus, OpenVAS, Bandit,
Checkmarx, SonarQube, Fortify, Veracode, Nikto, Nuclei, Nmap, Snyk, Prowler, Checkov, Gitleaks. Plus
existing: SARIF, CycloneDX, SPDX, VEX, Trivy, Grype, Semgrep, Dependabot.  If scanner_type is not
provided, auto-detection is used. Set pipeline=true to push findings into the Brain Pipeline
immediately.

**Request Body:** `UploadFile`

**Responses:**


**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

Receive scanner output via webhook (raw body).  Set up your CI/CD to POST scanner output directly:
curl -X POST https://aldeci/api/v1/scanner-ingest/webhook/zap \     -H "X-API-Key: $KEY" \     -H
"Content-Type: application/json" \     --data-binary @zap-report.json

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/sandbox/reachability

**Tags:** sandbox-verification

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run reachability probes inside Docker sandbox.  Instead of probing from the host (SSRF risk), all
network checks run in ephemeral Docker containers with resource limits.

**Request Body:** `ReachabilityProbeRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `targets` | list | Yes |  | Target URLs or host:port to probe |
| `cve_id` | str | No |  | CVE being checked for reachability |
| `asset_ids` | list | No | PydanticUndefined | Asset IDs for correlation |

**Responses:**


**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get EPSS scores for CVEs.  EPSS (Exploit Prediction Scoring System) provides probability scores for
CVE exploitation in the next 30 days.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `cve_ids` | query | Optional | No | Comma-separated CVE IDs to lookup |
| `min_score` | query | float | No | 0.0 |
| `limit` | query | int | No | 100 |

**200 OK** — `Dict`

### 1330. `GET` `/api/v1/feeds/exploit-confidence/{cve_id}`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/feeds/exploit-confidence/{cve_id}

**Tags:** feeds

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get exploit confidence score for a CVE.  Exploit confidence is calculated based on: - EPSS score
(25%) - KEV presence (30%) - Exploit availability (15%) - Metasploit module (10%) - Nuclei template
(5%) - Verified exploit (5%) - Threat actor use (10%)

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `cve_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Tags:** feeds

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get geo-weighted risk score for a CVE.  Risk scores are adjusted based on regional exploitation

| `cve_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

(active/inactive/authenticated/unauthenticated), which env vars to set, and registration URLs for
obtaining free API keys.  Required API keys: - NVD_API_KEY — register at
https://nvd.nist.gov/developers/request-an-api-key - OTX_API_KEY — register at
https://otx.alienvault.com/api - ABUSEIPDB_API_KEY — register at https://www.abuseipdb.com/register

### 1341. `POST` `/api/v1/feeds/refresh/all`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/feeds/refresh/all

**Tags:** feeds

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Refresh all primary feeds (EPSS, KEV, NVD, ExploitDB, OSV, GitHub Advisories).  Each feed can be
selectively enabled/disabled via query parameters.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `include_nvd` | query | bool | No | Include NVD feed |
| `include_exploitdb` | query | bool | No | Include ExploitDB feed |
| `include_osv` | query | bool | No | Include OSV feed |
| `include_github` | query | bool | No | Include GitHub Advisories |

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/brain/reload

**Tags:** knowledge-brain

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Reload the in-memory NetworkX graph from SQLite.  Call this after bulk-inserting edges directly into
the DB (e.g. via the build_brain_edges script) so that path queries and most-connected analytics
reflect the new topology.

### 1367. `GET` `/api/v1/brain/trends`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/brain/trends

**Tags:** knowledge-brain

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

[V3] Get vulnerability trend analysis from scan history.  Detects severity drift, CWE emergence,
vulnerability recurrence, and volume trends across historical scans. Returns security posture score
(0-100) with trend direction.  Query params:     org_id: Filter by organization (optional)
app_id: Filter by application (optional)

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | Optional | No | None |
| `app_id` | query | Optional | No | None |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1368. `POST` `/api/v1/brain/feedback`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/brain/feedback

**Tags:** knowledge-brain

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

[V3] Submit false-positive (or true-positive) feedback on a finding.  When a scanner+CWE or rule_id
pattern accumulates 3+ FP reports, future findings matching that pattern are auto-suppressed in the
brain pipeline (step 3b).  Body:     finding_id: Finding to classify     is_false_positive: True =
FP, False = confirmed TP     reason: Free-text justification     scanner: Scanner name (e.g.
'semgrep', 'snyk')     cwe_id: CWE identifier (e.g. 'CWE-79')     app_id: Application context
rule_id: Specific rule that fired

**Request Body:** `FPFeedbackRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  | Finding ID to provide feedback on |
| `is_false_positive` | bool | Yes |  | True if this is a false positive |
| `reason` | str | No |  | Reason for the classification |
| `scanner` | str | No |  | Scanner that produced the finding |
| `cwe_id` | str | No |  | CWE ID of the finding |
| `app_id` | str | No |  | Application ID |
| `org_id` | str | No |  | Organization ID |
| `rule_id` | str | No |  | Rule/check ID that fired |
| `title` | str | No |  | Finding title |
| `analyst` | str | No |  | Analyst who reviewed |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Description:**

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

clusters should be merged - merge_blocked: Block automatic merge of two clusters - split_cluster:
Split a cluster into separate findings

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `cluster_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `feedback_type` | Literal | Yes |  | merge_allowed, merge_blocked, or split_cluster |
| `target_cluster_id` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `reason` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `operator_id` | Optional | No | None |  |

**Tags:** deduplication

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Compare current run against a baseline to identify NEW/EXISTING/FIXED.  Returns findings categorized
as: - NEW: Present in current run but not in baseline - EXISTING: Present in both runs - FIXED:
Present in baseline but not in current run

**Request Body:** `BaselineComparisonRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `current_run_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `baseline_run_id` | str | Yes |  |  |

### 1408. `POST` `/api/v1/smart-dedup/deduplicate`

**Summary:** Run smart deduplication across all strategies

**Tags:** smart-dedup

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run all five dedup strategies on the supplied findings.  Strategies applied: - EXACT_CVE: same CVE
from different scanners - FUZZY_TITLE: similar titles via Levenshtein ratio - SAME_FILE_LINE: same
file + overlapping line range - CROSS_SCANNER: same issue from 2+ scanners - COMPONENT_VERSION: same
package@version  Returns groups, surviving canonical findings, duplicate count, and alert fatigue
score (0-100).

**Request Body:** `DeduplicateRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `findings` | List | Yes |  | List of finding dicts to deduplicate |
| `org_id` | str | No |  | Tenant / org identifier |
| `fuzzy_threshold` | float | No | 0.82 | Levenshtein ratio threshold for fuzzy title matching (0-1) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1409. `GET` `/api/v1/smart-dedup/groups`

**Summary:** List dedup groups

**Tags:** smart-dedup

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

List persisted DedupGroups with optional org and strategy filters.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | Optional | No | Filter by org |
| `strategy` | query | Optional | No | Filter by strategy: exact_cve \| fuzzy_title \| same_file_line \| cross_scanner \| component_version |
| `limit` | query | int | No | Max results |

**Responses:**

### 1429. `POST` `/api/v1/verify/fix`

**Summary:** Verify a single post-fix

**Tags:** Post-Fix Verification

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run the full MPTE post-fix verification suite against a proposed fix.  Performs six verification
suites: 1. **Static analysis** — regex + AST pattern removal check 2. **Regression check** — new CWE
/ anti-pattern detection 3. **Dependency safety** — version range validation 4. **MPTE re-scan** —
simulated exploit re-run 5. **Style preservation** — indentation + naming convention check 6. **Test
coverage** — test density impact estimate  Returns a `VerificationReport` with: - `verified` (bool)
— did the fix pass all checks? - `confidence` (float 0–1) — aggregate confidence score -
`mpte_retest_result` — exploit_blocked / exploit_still_possible / inconclusive - `safe_to_deploy` —
enterprise deployment recommendation - `detailed_checks` — per-check pass/fail with explanations

**Request Body:** `VerifyFixRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `finding_id` | str | No |  | Identifier of the original finding (e.g. FIND-0042) |
| `finding_type` | str | No | unknown | Vulnerability category: sql_injection \| xss \| buffer_overflow \| path_traversal \| command_injection \| deserialization \| ssrf \| open_redirect \| xxe \| ldap_injection \| xpath_injection \| ... |
| `severity` | str | No | high | Finding severity: critical \| high \| medium \| low |
| `original_code` | str | Yes |  | The vulnerable code before the fix was applied |
| `fixed_code` | str | Yes |  | The proposed fixed code to verify |
| `language` | str | Yes |  | Source language: python \| javascript \| typescript \| java \| go \| c \| csharp \| ruby \| php \| rust |
| `file_path` | Optional | No | None | Optional file path for additional context |
| `context_code` | Optional | No | None | Optional surrounding code for richer analysis |
| `dep_changes` | Optional | No | None | Optional dependency changes {package: new_version} introduced by the fix |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1430. `POST` `/api/v1/verify/bulk`

**Summary:** Batch-verify multiple fixes

**Tags:** Post-Fix Verification

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run post-fix verification for up to 20 fixes in a single request.  Each fix is verified
independently.  If `fail_fast` is true the batch stops at the first failed verification.  Returns an
array of VerificationReport objects plus aggregate summary.

**Request Body:** `BulkVerifyRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `fixes` | List | Yes |  | List of fix verification requests (max 20) |
| `fail_fast` | bool | No | False | Stop on first failed verification |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1431. `POST` `/api/v1/verify/regression`

**Summary:** Check fix for regression patterns only

**Tags:** Post-Fix Verification

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run only the regression-detection suite (Suite 2) against a proposed fix.  Checks for: - Anti-
patterns introduced by the fix (swallowed exceptions, linter suppressions) - Security controls
removed by the fix (auth checks, CSRF, rate limiting) - Code size / bloat regression  Returns a
lightweight report with just the regression checks.

**Request Body:** `RegressionCheckRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `original_code` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `fixed_code` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `language` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `finding_id` | str | No |  |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1432. `POST` `/api/v1/verify/mpte-retest`

**Summary:** Re-run MPTE exploit simulation after fix

**Tags:** Post-Fix Verification

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Re-run the MPTE exploit simulation (Suite 4) against the fixed code.  Simulates whether the
previously identified exploit payload would still succeed against the patched code by: 1. Checking
if vulnerable code patterns are still present 2. Checking if the correct fix mitigation pattern was
applied 3. Comparing exploit indicators in original vs fixed code  Returns: - `mpte_retest_result`:
"exploit_blocked" | "exploit_still_possible" | "inconclusive" | "not_applicable" - `check`: detailed
CheckResult with explanation - `exploit_blocked`: bool shorthand

**Request Body:** `MPTERetestRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `original_code` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `fixed_code` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `language` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `finding_type` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `finding_id` | str | No |  |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1437. `POST` `/api/v1/mitre/map-findings`

**Summary:** Map findings to MITRE ATT&CK techniques

**Tags:** MITRE ATT&CK

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Map a list of application vulnerability scanner findings to MITRE ATT&CK v14 techniques using CWE
IDs, CVE IDs, and text-based matching.  Returns the full ATT&CK mapping with technique details, kill
chain coverage, and technique frequency analysis across all findings.

**Request Body:** `MapFindingsRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `findings` | List | Yes |  | List of security findings to map (max 500) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `MapFindingsResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `session_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `mapped_at` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `total_findings` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `total_techniques` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `total_tactics_covered` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `coverage_percentage` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `all_techniques` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `technique_frequency` | Dict | Yes |  |  |
| `kill_chain_coverage` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `finding_results` | List | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1440. `POST` `/api/v1/mitre/navigator-json`

**Summary:** Generate MITRE ATT&CK Navigator layer JSON

**Tags:** MITRE ATT&CK

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Generate a MITRE ATT&CK Navigator layer JSON from scanner findings.  The returned JSON conforms to
the ATT&CK Navigator layer schema v4.5 and can be imported directly at https://mitre-
attack.github.io/attack-navigator/  Techniques are color-coded by confidence: - Red (#d32f2f): High
confidence ≥ 80% - Orange (#f57c00): Medium confidence 60–79% - Yellow (#fbc02d): Lower confidence <
60%

**Request Body:** `NavigatorLayerRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `findings` | List | Yes |  | Security findings to include in the Navigator layer |
| `layer_name` | str | No | FixOps Application Vulnerability Coverage | Display name for the ATT&CK Navigator layer |
| `description` | str | No | Auto-generated by FixOps MITRE ATT&CK Mapper | Layer description |

**Responses:**

across all 14 tactics with technique details and confidence scores. Useful for understanding the
attacker's potential kill chain given the discovered vulnerabilities.

| `total_findings` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `total_tactics_covered` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `total_tactics` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `coverage_percentage` | float | Yes |  |  |
| `kill_chain_coverage` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `summary` | Dict | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1445. `POST` `/api/v1/airgap/configure`

**Summary:** Configure air-gap mode settings

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Configure air-gap mode settings.  Accepts a partial update — only supplied fields are modified.
Persists the configuration to disk immediately.

**Request Body:** `AirGapConfigureRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `mode` | Optional | No | None | Air-gap mode: disabled \| detected \| configured \| enforced |
| `classification_level` | Optional | No | None | Classification level: UNCLASSIFIED \| CUI \| SECRET \| TOP SECRET |
| `allow_local_network` | Optional | No | None | Allow LAN traffic (e.g. for local Ollama/vLLM) |
| `allow_usb_import` | Optional | No | None | Allow data import from removable media |
| `fips_mode` | Optional | No | None | FIPS enforcement: disabled \| audit \| enforced |
| `llm_backend` | Optional | No | None | Local LLM backend: ollama \| vllm \| llamacpp \| huggingface_local \| none |
| `llm_endpoint` | Optional | No | None | URL for the local LLM API (e.g. http://localhost:11434) |
| `llm_model` | Optional | No | None | Model name to use (e.g. mistral:7b, llama3:8b) |
| `enabled_scanners` | Optional | No | None | List of scanner names to enable, or ['all'] for all 25 |
| `offline_data_paths` | Optional | No | None | Override paths for offline data (vuln_db, signatures, etc.) |
| `configured_by` | Optional | No | None | Operator/user making the configuration change |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error
