### 1447. `POST` `/api/v1/airgap/import/vuln-db`

**Summary:** Import offline vulnerability database

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Import an offline vulnerability database bundle (ZIP format).  The bundle must contain: -
`manifest.json` — metadata with version, source, and SHA-256 checksum - `vuln_db.json.gz` — gzip-
compressed NVD/CVE JSON feed  Suitable for importing NVD data snapshots delivered via USB or secure
transfer. The bundle checksum is verified before import (FIPS SHA-256).

**Request Body:** `ImportVulnDBRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `bundle_path` | str | Yes |  | Absolute path on the server to the vulnerability DB ZIP bundle |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Summary:** Export vulnerability database for transfer

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Export the local vulnerability database as a signed ZIP bundle.  The output bundle includes: -
`manifest.json` — metadata and SHA-256 integrity checksum - `vuln_db.json.gz` — the compressed
vulnerability feed  Use this to distribute the vulnerability DB to other air-gapped instances via
USB or secure offline transfer.

**Request Body:** `ExportVulnDBRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |

| `output_path` | str | Yes |  | Absolute output path for the exported ZIP bundle |

**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)

Import a STIX 2.1 threat intelligence bundle.  Accepts: - Raw STIX 2.1 JSON file (`.json`) - ZIP
archive containing a STIX bundle (`.zip`) - TAXII collection export  Classification data markings
are preserved. Suitable for receiving threat intelligence from other air-gapped instances or from

**Summary:** Export threat intelligence for air-gapped sharing

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Export stored threat intelligence as a signed STIX 2.1 bundle ZIP.  The exported bundle includes: -
STIX 2.1 objects (indicators, TTPs, threat actors, malware, etc.) - Classification data marking
(STIX marking-definition object) - Export metadata with SHA-256 integrity checksum  Use this to
transfer threat intelligence to another air-gapped FixOps instance via USB or secure offline
transfer.

**Request Body:** `ExportThreatIntelRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `output_path` | str | Yes |  | Absolute output path for the exported bundle |
| `classification` | Optional | No | None | Override classification level for this export |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)

**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1451. `GET` `/api/v1/airgap/classification`

**Summary:** Get current classification level

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get the current data classification level and associated banner.  Classification levels: -
**UNCLASSIFIED** — Standard unclassified data - **CUI** — Controlled Unclassified Information
(purple banner) - **SECRET** — Secret classification (red banner `//SECRET//`) - **TOP SECRET** —
Top Secret classification (orange banner `//TOP SECRET//`)  When classification is set above
UNCLASSIFIED, all API responses include a `_classification` banner field.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1452. `POST` `/api/v1/airgap/classification`

**Summary:** Set classification level

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Set the data classification level for all FixOps operations.  Valid levels: - `UNCLASSIFIED` - `CUI`
(Controlled Unclassified Information) - `SECRET` - `TOP SECRET`  Once set, all API responses will
include the appropriate classification banner in the `_classification` field. This setting is
persisted and survives restarts.  **Note**: Increasing the classification level is a privileged
operation. The `set_by` field should identify the authorized operator.

**Request Body:** `ClassificationSetRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `level` | str | Yes |  | Classification level: UNCLASSIFIED \| CUI \| SECRET \| TOP SECRET |
| `set_by` | str | No | admin | Identity of the operator setting classification |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1453. `GET` `/api/v1/airgap/fips/status`

**Summary:** FIPS 140-2/3 compliance status

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get the current FIPS 140-2/3 compliance status.  Returns: - Current FIPS enforcement mode
(disabled/audit/enforced) - Whether the Linux kernel has FIPS mode enabled
(`/proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled`) - List of approved and forbidden cryptographic algorithms - Any
compliance violations detected during the current session - A full compliance report  FIPS 140-2/3
approved algorithms: SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, SHA3-256, SHA3-384, SHA3-512, HMAC-SHA256, HMAC-
SHA384, HMAC-SHA512.  Forbidden algorithms: MD5, SHA-1, RC4, DES, 3DES, Blowfish.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1454. `POST` `/api/v1/airgap/updates/package`

**Summary:** Create offline update package

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Create an offline update package for distribution to air-gapped instances.  Package types: -
`vuln_db` — Vulnerability database update - `signatures` — Scanner signature updates -
`compliance_rules` — Compliance rule updates (CIS, NIST, etc.) - `llm_model` — Local LLM model
weights - `full_system` — Full FixOps system update  The package includes: - All specified content
files - Per-file SHA-256 checksums - Package manifest with type, version, and creation metadata  The
output ZIP can be transferred to air-gapped instances via USB or secure offline transfer and applied
with `POST /updates/apply`.

**Request Body:** `CreateUpdatePackageRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `package_type` | str | Yes |  | Type: vuln_db \| signatures \| compliance_rules \| llm_model \| full_system |
| `content_paths` | List | Yes |  | List of absolute server-side paths to include in the package |
| `version` | str | Yes |  | Package version string (e.g. 2024.11.1) |
| `output_path` | str | Yes |  | Absolute output path for the generated ZIP package |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1455. `POST` `/api/v1/airgap/updates/apply`

**Summary:** Apply offline update package

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Apply an offline update package to this FixOps instance.  The update package is verified before
application: 1. ZIP structure integrity check 2. Manifest presence and validity 3. Per-file SHA-256
checksum verification (FIPS-compliant)  If any checksum fails, the update is rejected and no files
are modified. Files are only written after all checks pass.  After a successful `vuln_db` update,
the vulnerability scanner will automatically use the new database for all subsequent scans.

**Request Body:** `ApplyUpdatePackageRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `package_path` | str | Yes |  | Absolute path on the server to the update ZIP package |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1456. `GET` `/api/v1/airgap/dependencies`

**Summary:** List external dependencies and their offline alternatives

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

List all external dependencies that FixOps normally relies on, along with their offline alternatives
for air-gapped deployments.  This endpoint is the primary reference for operators configuring a new
air-gapped FixOps installation. It shows: - What external services are used in connected mode - The
corresponding offline alternative for each - Whether an offline alternative is available - Whether
the dependency is required for core functionality  Use this to plan your air-gapped infrastructure
requirements.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `dependency_type` | query | Optional | No | Filter by type: api \| dns \| package_registry \| llm \| vuln_db |
| `required_only` | query | bool | No | Only return required dependencies |
| `offline_available` | query | Optional | No | Filter by offline availability |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1457. `POST` `/api/v1/airgap/detect-isolation`

**Summary:** Probe network for isolation detection

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run live network probes to detect air-gap isolation.  Probes: - TCP connectivity to well-known
internet hosts (Google DNS, Cloudflare, OpenDNS) - DNS resolution of external hostnames - HTTPS
reachability to external API endpoints  If all probes fail, the system is considered air-gapped and
the mode is automatically set to `detected`.  This is safe to run in an already-isolated environment
— all probes have short timeouts (≤2 seconds) and fail silently.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1458. `POST` `/api/v1/airgap/llm/probe`

**Summary:** Probe and configure local LLM backend

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Auto-detect and configure available local LLM backends.  Probes the following backends in order: 1.
**Ollama** at `http://localhost:11434` 2. **vLLM** at `http://localhost:8000` 3. **llama.cpp** at
`http://localhost:8080`  If a backend is found, it is configured automatically and the first
available model is selected. The configuration is persisted.  Run this after installing and starting
a local LLM service to enable AI-powered features in air-gapped mode.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

db/lookup/CVE-2021-44228`

**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)

mode, violations are logged but allowed.

| `min_score` | query | float | No | Minimum CVSS score |
| `max_score` | query | float | No | Maximum CVSS score |
| `year` | query | Optional | No | Filter by CVE publication year |

### 1467. `POST` `/api/v1/airgap/update/export`

**Summary:** Export sneakernet update package

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Create an encrypted and integrity-signed update package for sneakernet transfer.  The package uses
FIPS-compliant AES-256 encryption and HMAC-SHA256 signing. Transfer via USB or removable media to
other air-gapped instances and import with POST /update/import.  Package types: cve_db | sbom |
trustgraph_config | signatures | full_system

**Request Body:** `SneakernetExportRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `payload_files` | List | Yes |  | Absolute server-side paths of files to include in the package |
| `package_type` | str | Yes |  | Package type: cve_db \| sbom \| trustgraph_config \| signatures \| full_system |
| `version` | str | Yes |  | Semantic version string, e.g. 2025.01.1 |
| `encryption_key_hex` | str | Yes |  | 64-hex-char AES-256 key for encrypting the package |
| `classification` | str | No | UNCLASSIFIED | Classification level for the package |
| `output_path` | Optional | No | None | Override output file path |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1468. `POST` `/api/v1/airgap/update/import`

**Summary:** Import sneakernet update package

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Import and verify an encrypted sneakernet update package.  Verification steps: 1. Magic header check
2. HMAC-SHA256 payload integrity 3. AES-256 decryption 4. Per-file SHA-256 checksum verification  If
any check fails, the import is rejected with no files written.

**Request Body:** `SneakernetImportRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `package_path` | str | Yes |  | Absolute path to the .snk package file on the server |
| `encryption_key_hex` | str | Yes |  | 64-hex-char AES-256 key that was used when exporting |
| `extract_dir` | Optional | No | None | Override extraction directory |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1469. `GET` `/api/v1/airgap/network-check`

**Summary:** Verify network isolation — active egress check

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run an active network isolation verification.  Probes: - TCP egress to known external IPs (Google
DNS, Cloudflare, PyPI, AI APIs) - DNS resolution of external hostnames - HTTP/HTTPS egress to
external endpoints  All probes have short timeouts (≤1 second). A fully isolated system will report
is_isolated=true with all egress checks blocked.  IMPORTANT: This endpoint is read-only and safe to
call in production. It makes outbound connection ATTEMPTS (which should all fail in air-gap).

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1470. `GET` `/api/v1/airgap/validate`

**Summary:** Run deployment validation checklist

**Tags:** Air-Gap Operations

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run the full SCIF/air-gap deployment pre-flight checklist.  Validates: - Air-gap mode enabled - FIPS
140-2 mode active - Offline CVE database populated - Telemetry fully disabled - TrustGraph
configured local-only - Network isolation configured - Audit logging operational - Data directory
accessible - Classification level valid - Package registries blocked  Returns overall pass/fail plus
per-check detail with severity (ERROR/WARNING/INFO).

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `classification` | query | str | No | Classification level for this deployment |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

session maintains its own conversation history and context.

| `context` | Optional | No | None | Initial context (e.g., CVE IDs, asset IDs) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SessionResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `agent_type` | CopilotAgentType | Yes |  |  |
| `created_at` | datetime | Yes |  |  |
| `updated_at` | datetime | Yes |  |  |
| `message_count` | int | No | 0 |  |
| `context` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |

### 1503. `POST` `/api/v1/copilot/ask`

**Summary:** Ask a security question (Rachel Kim / Junior Developer)

**Tags:** copilot, copilot

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Stateless natural-language security Q&A backed by a built-in CWE knowledge base. No external LLM
required. Covers CWE-89, CWE-79, CWE-78, CWE-22, CWE-798, CWE-502, CWE-918, CWE-611 and more.

**Request Body:** `AskRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `question` | str | Yes |  | Natural-language security question |
| `context` | Optional | No | None | Optional structured context to improve answer relevance |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `AskResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `answer` | str | Yes |  | Plain-English explanation of the vulnerability or security insight |
| `references` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Authoritative external references |
| `suggested_fix` | str | No |  | Concrete remediation guidance or code snippet |
| `severity_context` | str | No | medium | Typical severity level: critical / high / medium / low |
| `related_findings` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Related findings from the current session (if context provided) |
| `matched_cwe` | Optional | No | None | CWE identifier that best matched the question |
| `source` | str | No | builtin_knowledge_base | Origin of the answer (builtin_knowledge_base \| graphrag_security_insight \| llm_enhanced) |
| `intent` | Optional | No | None | Detected security-ops intent (top_risks \| compliance \| threat_landscape \| attack_surface) |
| `recommended_actions` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Recommended follow-up actions with API endpoints |
| `confidence` | float | No | 0.0 | Answer confidence score (0.0-1.0); higher when GraphRAG found relevant entities |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1537. `POST` `/api/v1/predictions/attack-chain`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/predictions/attack-chain

**Tags:** predictions

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Predict attack chain progression for a specific CVE.  Uses Markov Chain modeling based on MITRE
ATT&CK kill chain to predict likely attack progression and time-to-impact.

**Request Body:** `AttackChainRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `cve_id` | str | Yes |  | CVE identifier |
| `cvss_score` | float | No | 7.5 | CVSS score (0-10) |
| `has_exploit` | bool | No | False | Whether an exploit is available |
| `is_network_exposed` | bool | No | True | Whether vulnerability is network-accessible |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/predictions/risk-trajectory

**Tags:** predictions

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Calculate risk trajectory over time horizon.  Computes n-step transition probabilities to predict
risk evolution.

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `current_state` | str | No | Initial | Current security state |
| `horizon_steps` | int | No | 10 | Number of steps to predict |

**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1543. `POST` `/api/v1/predictions/bayesian/risk-assessment`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/predictions/bayesian/risk-assessment

**Tags:** predictions

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Assess vulnerability risk using Bayesian Network model.  Uses pgmpy-based Bayesian Network with
SSVC-inspired factors to compute risk probability distribution.

**Request Body:** `VulnerabilityRiskRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `exploitation` | str | No | none | Exploitation status: none, poc, active |
| `exposure` | str | No | controlled | Exposure level: controlled, limited, open |
| `utility` | str | No | laborious | Attack utility: laborious, efficient, super_effective |
| `safety_impact` | str | No | negligible | Safety impact: negligible, marginal, major, hazardous |
| `mission_impact` | str | No | degraded | Mission impact: degraded, crippled, mev |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1553. `POST` `/api/v1/algorithms/monte-carlo/quantify`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/algorithms/monte-carlo/quantify

**Tags:** ALdeci Algorithms

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Quantify risk using FAIR-based Monte Carlo simulation.  Returns Value-at-Risk (VaR), Expected Annual
Loss (ALE), loss exceedance probabilities, and confidence intervals.

**Request Body:** `MonteCarloRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `threat_event_frequency_min` | float | No | 0.1 | Min annual threat events |
| `threat_event_frequency_mode` | float | No | 1.0 | Most likely annual threat events |
| `threat_event_frequency_max` | float | No | 5.0 | Max annual threat events |
| `vulnerability_probability_min` | float | No | 0.1 | Min probability of successful exploit |
| `vulnerability_probability_mode` | float | No | 0.5 | Most likely probability |
| `vulnerability_probability_max` | float | No | 0.9 | Max probability |
| `loss_magnitude_min` | float | No | 10000 | Minimum loss in dollars |
| `loss_magnitude_mode` | float | No | 100000 | Most likely loss |
| `loss_magnitude_max` | float | No | 1000000 | Maximum loss |
| `iterations` | int | No | 10000 | Number of simulations |
| `confidence_level` | float | No | 0.95 | Confidence level for intervals |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1554. `POST` `/api/v1/algorithms/monte-carlo/cve`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/algorithms/monte-carlo/cve

**Tags:** ALdeci Algorithms

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Quantify risk for a specific CVE using Monte Carlo simulation.

**Request Body:** `CVERiskRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `cve_id` | str | Yes |  | CVE identifier |
| `cvss_score` | float | No | 5.0 | CVSS score (default 5.0 if unknown) |
| `epss_score` | float | No | 0.0 | EPSS score (0-1) |
| `kev_listed` | bool | No | False | Whether in CISA KEV catalog |
| `asset_value` | float | No | 100000 | Asset value in dollars |
| `is_reachable` | bool | No | True | Whether vulnerable code is reachable |
| `simulations` | int | No | 10000 | Number of simulations |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1556. `POST` `/api/v1/algorithms/causal/analyze`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/algorithms/causal/analyze

**Tags:** ALdeci Algorithms

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Perform causal inference analysis on a vulnerability.  Identifies root causes, contributing factors,
and provides SHAP-like explanations for risk factors.

**Request Body:** `CausalAnalysisRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `has_exploit` | bool | No | False | Whether an exploit is available |
| `is_reachable` | bool | No | True | Whether vulnerable code is reachable |
| `is_internet_facing` | bool | No | False | Whether exposed to internet |
| `has_waf` | bool | No | False | Whether WAF is enabled |
| `is_patched` | bool | No | False | Whether vulnerability is patched |
| `has_auth` | bool | No | True | Whether authentication is required |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

| `has_auth` | bool | No | True | Whether authentication is required |

**Summary:** Scan prompt for threats

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Scan an input prompt for injection attacks, secrets, and invisible text.  Returns whether the prompt
was blocked and any issues detected.

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|

| `fail_fast` | bool | No | True | Stop on first detected issue |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `ScanResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `blocked` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `issues` | list | Yes |  |  |
| `sanitized_text` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `method` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `scanner_scores` | Dict | No | {} |  |
| `scan_time_ms` | float | No | 0.0 |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/vllm/test-inference

**Tags:** Self-Hosted LLM (Air-Gapped)

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Test inference round-trip to verify self-hosted LLM is working.  Sends a test prompt and returns the
response with timing metrics. Useful for verifying air-gapped setup before demo.

**Request Body:** `TestInferenceRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `prompt` | str | No | Explain SQL injection in one sentence. | Test prompt to send to the self-hosted LLM |
| `backend` | Optional | No | None | Backend to test: vllm, ollama, or auto |

**200 OK** — `Dict`

| `decision_id` | str | Yes |  | AI decision ID |
| `finding_id` | str | Yes |  | Finding ID |
| `predicted_action` | str | Yes |  | What AI decided |
| `actual_outcome` | str | Yes |  | What actually happened |
| `confidence` | float | No | 0.0 |  |
| `context` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1638. `POST` `/api/v1/self-learning/score-with-learning`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/self-learning/score-with-learning

**Tags:** Self-Learning

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Score a finding showing before/after learning effect.  This is the key demo endpoint: it shows how
the same vulnerability gets a different risk score after the system has learned from past decisions,
FP feedback, MPTE verifications, and remediation outcomes.  Returns:     - baseline_score: Score
without learning     - adjusted_score: Score with learning adjustments     - delta: The difference
(negative = lower risk after learning)     - adjustments: Detailed breakdown of each learning factor

**Request Body:** `ScoreWithLearningRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `cvss_score` | float | No | 7.5 | CVSS base score |
| `epss_score` | float | No | 0.3 | EPSS probability |
| `in_kev` | bool | No | False | In CISA KEV catalog? |
| `asset_criticality` | float | No | 0.7 | Asset criticality |
| `scanner` | str | No | semgrep | Scanner that found this |
| `rule_id` | str | No | CWE-89-sql-injection | Rule/check ID |
| `fix_type` | str | No | CODE_PATCH | Expected fix type |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1639. `POST` `/api/v1/self-learning/compute-adjustments`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/self-learning/compute-adjustments

**Tags:** Self-Learning

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Run the learning step: analyze feedback → compute weight adjustments.  This is the "brain learns"
step. It analyzes all feedback data, identifies patterns, and adjusts internal weights that modify
future risk scoring. Each adjustment is logged with reasoning.  Returns:     - adjustments: List of
weight changes with old/new values and reasoning     - count: Number of adjustments applied

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1640. `GET` `/api/v1/self-learning/weights`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/self-learning/weights

**Tags:** Self-Learning

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get all learned weights.  Shows every weight that the self-learning engine has computed. Weights
modify risk scoring — values < 1.0 reduce risk scores, values > 1.0 increase them.  If no weights
exist yet, triggers compute_adjustments() automatically using the existing feedback data.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

Seed realistic demo data for all 5 feedback loops.  Populates the learning database with 98

adjustments and score-with-learning.

**Tags:** Self-Learning, admin, demo

Reset all learning data for a fresh demo.  Clears all feedback records, weight adjustments, and

### 1645. `GET` `/api/v1/self-learning/demo/full-loop`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/self-learning/demo/full-loop

**Tags:** Self-Learning, admin, demo

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Execute a complete self-learning demo in one call.  This endpoint demonstrates the FULL feedback
loop: 1. Reset any existing data 2. Score a sample finding (baseline — no learning) 3. Seed demo
feedback data (98 records across 5 loops) 4. Run learning step (compute weight adjustments) 5. Score
the SAME finding again (now with learning adjustments) 6. Show the improvement (delta between
baseline and adjusted)  This is the demo that proves ALdeci gets smarter with every decision.

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1646. `POST` `/api/v1/self-learning/demo/live-feedback`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/self-learning/demo/live-feedback

**Tags:** Self-Learning, admin, demo

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Submit one feedback record, run learning, and show scoring effect.  This is the interactive demo
endpoint: submit a single feedback item, immediately compute adjustments, and show how the risk
score changed.  Each call adds to the learning history — call multiple times to see the cumulative
learning effect.  Steps per call: 1. Score the finding BEFORE this feedback (current state) 2.
Record the feedback for the specified loop 3. Re-compute weight adjustments 4. Score the finding
AFTER learning 5. Return before/after comparison

**Request Body:** `LiveFeedbackRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `loop` | str | Yes |  | Loop name: decision, mpte, fp, remediation, policy |
| `decision_id` | str | No |  | Decision ID (for decision loop) |
| `finding_id` | str | No |  | Finding ID |
| `predicted_action` | str | No | FIX | What AI decided |
| `actual_outcome` | str | No | FIX | What actually happened |
| `predicted_exploitable` | bool | No | True | Was it predicted exploitable? |
| `actual_exploitable` | bool | No | True | Was it actually exploitable? |
| `mpte_confidence` | float | No | 0.8 |  |
| `scanner` | str | No | semgrep | Scanner name |
| `rule_id` | str | No | CWE-89 | Rule ID |
| `is_false_positive` | bool | No | False | Is this a false positive? |
| `fix_type` | str | No | CODE_PATCH | Fix type |
| `fix_applied` | str | No | Applied fix | Fix description |
| `resolved` | bool | No | True | Did the fix resolve the issue? |
| `time_to_fix_hours` | float | No | 2.0 |  |
| `policy_id` | str | No | POL-001 | Policy ID |
| `violated` | bool | No | True | Was the policy violated? |
| `was_justified` | bool | No | False | Was the violation justified? |
| `cvss_score` | float | No | 7.5 |  |
| `epss_score` | float | No | 0.35 |  |
| `in_kev` | bool | No | False |  |
| `asset_criticality` | float | No | 0.7 |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `mode` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `block_sqli` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `block_xss` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `block_cmdi` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `block_path_traversal` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `block_ssrf` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `block_prototype_pollution` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `block_deserialization` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `block_bots` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `block_zero_day_patterns` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `rate_limit_rpm` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `bot_score_threshold` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `ip_allowlist` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `ip_denylist` | Optional | No | None |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

on integration type: - Jira/ServiceNow/GitLab/GitHub/Azure DevOps: Validates connection and syncs
metadata - Slack: Tests webhook connectivity - Confluence: Validates space access -
Snyk/SonarQube/Dependabot: Validates security tool connectivity - AWS Security Hub/Azure Security
Center: Validates cloud security connectivity

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/webhooks/outbox/{outbox_id}/execute

**Tags:** webhooks

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Execute an outbox item by calling the appropriate connector.  This endpoint actually delivers the
outbox item to the external system using the configured connectors. It handles retry logic and
updates the outbox item status based on the result.

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|

### 1712. `POST` `/api/v1/webhooks/outbox/process-pending`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/webhooks/outbox/process-pending

**Tags:** webhooks

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Process pending outbox items that are ready for delivery.  This endpoint processes up to `limit`
pending outbox items that have either never been attempted or whose next_retry_at time has passed.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `limit` | query | int | No | 10 |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1713. `POST` `/api/v1/webhooks/alm/work-items`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/webhooks/alm/work-items

**Tags:** webhooks

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Queue creation of a work item in an ALM system.  This endpoint queues the work item creation in the
outbox for reliable delivery. The actual creation happens asynchronously via the outbox processor.

**Request Body:** `CreateWorkItemRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `cluster_id` | str | No | default-cluster |  |
| `integration_type` | Literal | No | jira |  |
| `title` | str | No | Untitled Work Item |  |
| `description` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `severity` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `labels` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `assignee` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `project_id` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `additional_fields` | Optional | No | None |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1736. `GET` `/api/v1/analytics/trends`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/analytics/trends

**Tags:** analytics-dashboard

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return finding counts (new, resolved, reopened, total_open) bucketed by the requested granularity
over the specified date range.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | str | No | Organisation identifier |
| `granularity` | query | TimeGranularity | No | Time bucket size |
| `start` | query | Optional | No | Start date (ISO 8601) |
| `end` | query | Optional | No | End date (ISO 8601) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `List`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

| `false_positive_rate_percent` | float | Yes |  | False Positive Rate |
| `findings_critical` | int | Yes |  | Critical findings |
| `findings_high` | int | Yes |  | High findings |
| `connector_uptime_percent` | float | Yes |  | Connector uptime |
| `council_consensus_percent` | float | Yes |  | LLM council consensus |
| `sla_compliance_percent` | float | Yes |  | SLA compliance |

### 1749. `POST` `/api/v1/analytics/record`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/analytics/record

**Tags:** analytics

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Record a custom metric data point.  Metrics are stored in time-series database for aggregation and
trending.  Parameters: - metric_name: Metric name - value: Numeric value - unit: Unit of measurement
(optional) - dimensions: Dimensional breakdown (optional) - timestamp: Data point time (optional,
defaults to now)  Returns: Metric ID

**Request Body:** `MetricRecordRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `metric_name` | str | Yes |  | Metric name |
| `value` | float | Yes |  | Metric value |
| `unit` | str | No |  | Unit of measurement |
| `metric_type` | str | No | value | Metric type |
| `dimensions` | Optional | No | None | Dimensional breakdown |
| `timestamp` | Optional | No | None | Data point timestamp |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

Create a new API key. The plaintext key is returned ONCE.

|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `role` | str | No | viewer |  |
| `scopes` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `expires_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `rate_limit` | int | No | 60 |  |
| `description` | str | No |  |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `CreateKeyResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `prefix` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `org_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `created_by` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `created_at` | datetime | Yes |  |  |
| `expires_at` | Optional | Yes |  |  |
| `last_used_at` | Optional | Yes |  |  |
| `use_count` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `rate_limit` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `scopes` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `role` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `is_active` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `description` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `raw_key` | str | Yes |  | Store this securely — shown only once |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1770. `POST` `/api/v1/backup-dr/jobs`

**Summary:** Register backup job

**Tags:** backup-dr

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Create or update a backup job definition.

**Request Body:** `RegisterBackupJobRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Descriptive name for the backup job |
| `system_name` | str | Yes |  | Logical system being backed up |
| `backup_type` | BackupType | No | BackupType.FULL | Backup type |
| `source_path` | str | Yes |  | Source path, DB name, or bucket URI |
| `destination` | str | Yes |  | Destination storage URI or path |
| `schedule_cron` | str | Yes |  | Cron expression (e.g. '0 2 * * *') |
| `retention_days` | int | No | 30 | Retention period in days |
| `encryption` | EncryptionType | No | EncryptionType.AES256 | Encryption in transit/at rest |
| `status` | BackupStatus | No | BackupStatus.ACTIVE | Current job status |
| `last_run_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `last_run_size_bytes` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `next_run_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `tags` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `metadata` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Organisation ID |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `BackupJob`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | str | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `system_name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `backup_type` | BackupType | No | BackupType.FULL |  |
| `source_path` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `destination` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `schedule_cron` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `retention_days` | int | No | 30 |  |
| `encryption` | EncryptionType | No | EncryptionType.AES256 |  |
| `status` | BackupStatus | No | BackupStatus.ACTIVE |  |
| `last_run_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `last_run_size_bytes` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `last_run_duration_seconds` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `next_run_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `tags` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `metadata` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `org_id` | str | No | default |  |
| `created_at` | str | No | PydanticUndefined |  |

### 1778. `POST` `/api/v1/backup-dr/verifications`

**Summary:** Record backup verification

**Tags:** backup-dr

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Record an integrity check / restore test result for a backup artifact.

**Request Body:** `RecordVerificationRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `backup_job_id` | str | Yes |  | Backup job this verification covers |
| `backup_artifact_path` | str | Yes |  | Path or URI to the backup artifact |
| `sha256_checksum` | Optional | No | None | SHA-256 hash of the artifact |
| `checksum_verified` | bool | No | False | Was the checksum validated? |
| `restore_tested` | bool | No | False | Was a restore test performed? |
| `restore_test_result` | VerificationStatus | No | VerificationStatus.PENDING |  |
| `restore_test_duration_seconds` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `backup_age_hours` | Optional | No | None | Age of the backup in hours |
| `age_alert_threshold_hours` | float | No | 48.0 | Hours before age alert fires |
| `verified_by` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `notes` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `org_id` | str | No | default | Organisation ID |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `BackupVerification`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|

| `restore_test_duration_seconds` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `backup_age_hours` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `age_alert_triggered` | bool | No | False |  |
| `age_alert_threshold_hours` | float | No | 48.0 |  |
| `verified_at` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `verified_by` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `notes` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `org_id` | str | No | default |  |

### 1802. `POST` `/api/v1/changelog/generate`

**Summary:** Generate changelog from commits

**Tags:** changelog

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Parse the provided commit log text and return a formatted changelog.  Accepts conventional commits
(``feat:``, ``fix:``, ...) and beast-mode commits (``beast-mode(type): ...``).

**Request Body:** `GenerateRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `commits` | str | Yes |  | Raw commit log text. Each line may be plain commit messages or tabular format: <sha>\t<author>\t<date>\t<message> |
| `version` | str | No | 1.0.0 | Version label for this changelog |
| `format` | OutputFormat | No | OutputFormat.MARKDOWN | Output format |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `GenerateResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `version` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `format` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `content` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `entry_count` | int | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1803. `GET` `/api/v1/changelog/unreleased`

**Summary:** Changes since last tag

**Tags:** changelog

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return all unreleased commits since the given tag, with a suggested next version based on change
types.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `since_tag` | query | str | No | Git tag to diff from (empty = all commits) |
| `current_version` | query | str | No | Current version for bump suggestion |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `UnreleasedResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `since_tag` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `entry_count` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `entries` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `suggested_version` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1804. `POST` `/api/v1/changelog/suggest-version`

**Summary:** Suggest next semver

**Tags:** changelog

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Parse commits and suggest the appropriate next semantic version.  Rules: - Breaking change → major
bump - New feature → minor bump - Bug fix / other → patch bump

**Request Body:** `SuggestVersionRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `commits` | str | Yes |  | Raw commit log text |
| `current_version` | str | No | 0.0.0 | Current semver string |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SuggestVersionResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `current_version` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `suggested_version` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `bump_type` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `entry_count` | int | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 1805. `POST` `/api/v1/changelog/release-notes`

**Summary:** Generate release notes

**Tags:** changelog

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Generate concise, human-readable release notes from a commit list.  The notes highlight breaking
changes, summarise feature/fix counts, and list the top highlights.

**Request Body:** `ReleaseNotesRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `commits` | str | Yes |  | Raw commit log text |
| `version` | str | No | 1.0.0 | Version label |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `ReleaseNotesResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `version` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `notes` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Auth:** API Key required

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Metric identifier |
| `value` | float | Yes |  | Numeric metric value |
| `unit` | str | No |  | Unit label (e.g. 'score', 'count', '%') |
| `category` | MetricCategory | Yes |  | Metric category |
| `trend` | MetricTrend | No | MetricTrend.STABLE | Trend direction |
| `change_pct` | float | No | 0.0 | Percentage change vs previous period |
| `period` | str | No | current | Period label |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

| `user` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `details` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `threat_level` | ThreatLevel | No | ThreatLevel.NONE |  |

### 1941. `PUT` `/api/v1/rate-limits/config`

**Summary:** Update endpoint tier mapping or per-key rate limit override

**Tags:** rate-limits

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Update endpoint tier mapping and/or per-key request limit.  - Provide ``path_pattern`` + ``tier`` to
register a new endpoint mapping. - Provide ``api_key_id`` + ``requests_per_minute`` to set a per-key
override   (set ``requests_per_minute`` to 0 to remove the override).

**Request Body:** `EndpointConfigUpdate`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `path_pattern` | Optional | No | None | Regex pattern for the endpoint path (e.g. '^/api/v1/custom'). |
| `tier` | Optional | No | None | Rate limit tier to assign to the path pattern. |
| `api_key_id` | Optional | No | None | API key ID to apply a per-key request-per-minute override. |
| `requests_per_minute` | Optional | No | None | Requests per minute for the per-key override. 0 removes override. |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Description:**

Clear all sliding window entries for the given API key ID.  This allows the key to make requests
immediately, regardless of prior usage.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `api_key_id` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `ResetResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `status` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `api_key_id` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 1977. `POST` `/api/v1/slack/commands`

**Summary:** Slack slash command handler

**Tags:** Slack

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Handle a Slack slash command (e.g. /status, /findings, /sla).  Slack sends form-encoded data; this
endpoint decodes it, optionally verifies the HMAC signature, and returns a BlockKit response.

**Request Body:** `str`

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `SlackCommandResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `response_type` | str | No | ephemeral | 'in_channel' or 'ephemeral' |
| `blocks` | list | No | PydanticUndefined |  |
| `text` | Optional | No | None |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

| `entity_id` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `entity_name` | str | No |  |  |
| `period_label` | str | No |  | e.g. '2026-Q1' |
| `dimensions` | List | No | PydanticUndefined |  |

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/security-scorecard/scorecards/domain

**Tags:** security-scorecard-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Generate a scorecard from 6 weighted domain scores.  Domains: identity(20%), endpoint(20%),
network(15%), cloud(15%), data(15%), application(15%).

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | str | No | default |

**Request Body:** `DomainScorecardCreate`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `identity` | float | No | 0.0 |  |
| `endpoint` | float | No | 0.0 |  |
| `network` | float | No | 0.0 |  |
| `cloud` | float | No | 0.0 |  |
| `data` | float | No | 0.0 |  |
| `application` | float | No | 0.0 |  |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 2057. `POST` `/api/v1/ingest/multipart`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/ingest/multipart

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Scanner-agnostic multipart ingestion endpoint.  Accepts multiple files in various formats (SARIF,
CycloneDX, SPDX, VEX, CNAPP, dark web intel, etc.) and normalizes them into a unified Finding model.
Features: - Auto-detection of format variants - Parallel processing for multiple files - Format
drift handling with lenient parsing - Performance: 10K findings in <2 min  Args:     files: One or
more files to ingest     format_hint: Optional format hint (sarif, cyclonedx, spdx, vex, cnapp,
dark_web_intel)  Returns:     Ingestion results with normalized findings and asset inventory

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `format_hint` | query | Optional | No | None |

**Request Body:** `List`

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 2070. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/ingest`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/ingest

**Tags:** connectors

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Ingest normalized findings from a connector.  This is the primary entry point for n8n workflows and
custom connectors to submit findings in ALDECI's canonical format. Validates schema, performs
deduplication via content hash, and routes through the pipeline in a background task.  Args:
payload: IngestPayload with source, findings list, and metadata     background_tasks: FastAPI
background task executor     org_id: Organization ID (from API key auth)  Returns:     IngestResult
with accepted/duplicate/error counts and job ID  Status Codes:     202 Accepted: Findings queued for
processing     400 Bad Request: Invalid payload     401 Unauthorized: Invalid API key     429 Too
Many Requests: Rate limit exceeded     500 Internal Server Error: Pipeline error

**Request Body:** `IngestPayload`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `source` | str | Yes |  | Connector name (e.g., 'github', 'jira') |
| `findings` | List | Yes |  | List of normalized findings |
| `metadata` | ConnectorIngestMeta | Yes |  | Ingest metadata |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `IngestResult`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `ingest_id` | str | Yes |  | Unique ID for this ingest batch |
| `source` | str | Yes |  | Connector source name |
| `timestamp` | datetime | Yes |  | When ingest was processed |
| `accepted_count` | int | Yes |  | Number of findings accepted |
| `duplicate_count` | int | Yes |  | Number of duplicates skipped |
| `error_count` | int | Yes |  | Number of parsing errors |
| `errors` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Error details |
| `job_id` | str | Yes |  | Background pipeline job ID |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 2071. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/ingest/raw`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/ingest/raw

**Tags:** connectors

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Ingest raw scanner output files.  Accepts scanner files in various formats (JSON, XML, SARIF, etc.)
and routes through DefectDojo for parsing into normalized findings. Supports multipart file upload.
Args:     file: Scanner output file (max 100MB)     scan_type: Scanner type (e.g., 'sarif', 'json',
'nessus', 'burp')     product_name: Product/project name for grouping     org_id: Organization ID
Returns:     RawIngestResult with parsed finding count and DefectDojo import ID  Status Codes:
202 Accepted: File queued for parsing     400 Bad Request: Invalid scan_type or product_name     413
Payload Too Large: File exceeds 100MB limit     422 Unprocessable Entity: File parsing failed
500 Internal Server Error: DefectDojo error

**Request Body:** `UploadFile`

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `RawIngestResult`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `ingest_id` | str | Yes |  | Unique ID for this raw ingest |
| `scan_type` | str | Yes |  | Scanner type (e.g. 'sarif', 'json') |
| `product_name` | str | Yes |  | Product/project name |
| `timestamp` | datetime | Yes |  | When ingest was processed |
| `parsed_findings_count` | int | Yes |  | Number of findings parsed |
| `errors` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | Parsing errors |
| `defectdojo_import_id` | Optional | No | None | DefectDojo import ID (if applicable) |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 2073. `GET` `/api/v1/connectors/{name}/health`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/connectors/{name}/health

**Tags:** connectors

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get health status for a specific connector.  Performs a health check on the connector (if
applicable) and returns current status, last error, and diagnostic details.  Args:     name:
Connector name     org_id: Organization ID  Returns:     ConnectorHealth with status and details
Status Codes:     200 OK: Health check succeeded     404 Not Found: Connector not found

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `name` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `ConnectorHealth`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Connector name |
| `status` | ConnectorStatus | Yes |  | Health status |
| `timestamp` | datetime | Yes |  | Status check timestamp |
| `details` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined | Status details |
| `last_error` | Optional | No | None | Last error message (if unhealthy) |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 2074. `POST` `/api/v1/connectors/{name}/pull`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/connectors/{name}/pull

**Tags:** connectors

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Trigger an on-demand pull for a specific connector.  Queues an async pull job for the connector. Can
optionally pull only findings modified since a specific timestamp (for incremental pulls).  Args:
name: Connector name     request: Optional PullJobRequest with since parameter     background_tasks:
FastAPI background task executor     org_id: Organization ID  Returns:     PullJobResponse with job
ID and estimated completion time  Status Codes:     202 Accepted: Pull job queued     404 Not Found:
Connector not found     409 Conflict: Another pull already in progress     500 Internal Server
Error: Pull error

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `name` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Request Body:** `Optional`

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `PullJobResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `job_id` | str | Yes |  | Async pull job ID |
| `connector` | str | Yes |  | Connector name |
| `timestamp` | datetime | Yes |  | When pull was triggered |
| `expected_completion_seconds` | Optional | No | None | Estimated seconds until completion |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 2075. `GET` `/api/v1/connectors/stages/{stage}`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/connectors/stages/{stage}

**Tags:** connectors

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

List connectors registered for a specific SDLC stage.  Returns all connectors that cover the given
SDLC stage (e.g., source-code, build, artifact, deploy-pre, runtime, external-supply-chain).  Args:
stage: SDLC stage     org_id: Organization ID  Returns:     List of ConnectorMetadata for connectors
covering the stage

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `stage` | path | SDLCStage | Yes | — |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `List`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 2076. `GET` `/api/v1/connectors/findings/pending-export`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/connectors/findings/pending-export

**Tags:** connectors

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get findings pending export to external systems (bidirectional workflows).  Returns findings that
are ready to be exported to external systems (Jira, GitHub, Slack). Used by n8n bidirectional
workflows to keep external systems in sync.  Args:     target: Target system for export (jira,
github, slack)     limit: Max findings to return     since: Only return findings modified after this
time     org_id: Organization ID  Returns:     FindingsForExportResponse with list of findings ready
for export

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `target` | query | FindingExportTarget | Yes | Target system (jira, github, slack) |
| `limit` | query | int | No | Max findings to return |
| `since` | query | Optional | No | Findings modified since this time |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `FindingsForExportResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `target` | FindingExportTarget | Yes |  | Target system |
| `findings` | List | Yes |  | Findings ready to export |
| `total_count` | int | Yes |  | Total pending for target |
| `since` | datetime | Yes |  | Findings modified since this time |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 2077. `GET` `/api/v1/connectors/metrics`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/connectors/metrics

**Tags:** connectors

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Get aggregated connector metrics (pulls, findings, error rates).  Returns per-connector metrics for
the last 24h and 7d, including pull counts, error rates, findings ingested, and average pull
duration. Used for monitoring connector health and throughput.  Args:     org_id: Organization ID
Returns:     ConnectorMetricsResponse with per-connector metrics

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `ConnectorMetricsResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `timestamp` | datetime | Yes |  | When metrics were computed |
| `metrics` | List | Yes |  | Per-connector metrics |
| `total_pulls_24h` | int | Yes |  | Total pulls across all connectors |
| `total_findings_ingested_24h` | int | Yes |  | Total findings ingested |
| `overall_error_rate` | float | Yes |  | Overall error rate |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 2084. `GET` `/api/v1/mcp/tools`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/mcp/tools

**Tags:** mcp-discovery

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return the complete MCP tool catalog with optional filtering.  This endpoint returns auto-discovered
tools generated from all FastAPI routes registered in the application. Tools are generated once at
startup and cached for performance.  Supports filtering by category (query/action/analysis), tag,
HTTP method, and free-text search across tool names and descriptions.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `category` | query | Optional | No | Filter by category: query, action, analysis |
| `tag` | query | Optional | No | Filter by tag |
| `method` | query | Optional | No | Filter by HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH |
| `search` | query | Optional | No | Search tool names and descriptions |
| `deprecated` | query | Optional | No | Filter by deprecation status |
| `limit` | query | int | No | Max tools to return |
| `offset` | query | int | No | Offset for pagination |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `List`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 2085. `GET` `/api/v1/mcp/tools/{tool_name}`

**Summary:** GET /api/v1/mcp/tools/{tool_name}

**Tags:** mcp-discovery

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return the schema for a single MCP tool by name.  Raises 404 if the tool does not exist in the
catalog.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `tool_name` | path | str | Yes | — |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `MCPToolDefinition`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | str | Yes |  | Unique tool name derived from the route's endpoint function name |
| `description` | str | No |  | Human-readable description from the endpoint docstring |
| `inputSchema` | MCPToolInputSchema | No | PydanticUndefined | JSON Schema for tool input parameters |
| `method` | str | Yes |  | HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) |
| `path` | str | Yes |  | API route path |
| `tags` | List | No | PydanticUndefined | OpenAPI tags |
| `category` | str | No | query | Tool category: query, action, or analysis |
| `requires_auth` | bool | No | True | Whether the endpoint requires auth |
| `deprecated` | bool | No | False | Whether the route is deprecated |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

### 2086. `POST` `/api/v1/mcp/execute`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/mcp/execute

**Tags:** mcp-discovery

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Execute an MCP tool by name with the given arguments.  This endpoint resolves the tool name to its
backing FastAPI route, constructs the appropriate HTTP request, and forwards it internally. The
result is returned in a standardized MCP response envelope.  Note: This is a proxy endpoint. The
actual execution happens by forwarding to the underlying route handler. Authentication and
authorization are inherited from the caller's session.

**Request Body:** `MCPExecuteRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `tool_name` | str | Yes |  | The tool name to execute |
| `arguments` | Dict | No | PydanticUndefined | Arguments matching the tool's inputSchema |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `MCPExecuteResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `tool_name` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `method` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `path` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `status` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `status_code` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `result` | Any | No | None |  |
| `error` | Optional | No | None |  |
| `execution_time_ms` | float | No | 0.0 |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Return all tool schemas in MCP-compliant format.  The 'mcp' format returns the standard MCP

returns a simplified OpenAPI-style schema.

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

GraphRAG retrieval: find entities relevant to a query and traverse their relationships to build LLM-
ready context.  - **query**: Natural language security question or topic - **top_k**: How many seed
entities to find (default 10) - **hops**: How many relationship hops to traverse (0-3, default 2)

**Request Body:** `RetrieveRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | str | Yes |  | Natural language security query |
| `top_k` | int | No | 10 | Max seed entities |
| `hops` | int | No | 2 | Relationship traversal depth |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `RetrieveResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `query` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `entities` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `relationships` | List | Yes |  |  |
| `context_summary` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `retrieval_method` | str | Yes |  |  |

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

|------|----|------|----------|-------|

**Request Body:** `EscalationPolicyRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|

| `severity_bump` | bool | No | False | Whether to bump severity on escalation |

| `breach_threshold_hours` | int | Yes |  |  |
| `auto_action` | str | Yes |  |  |
| `severity_bump` | bool | Yes |  |  |
| `updated_at` | Optional | No | None |  |

**Summary:** Asset-vulnerability correlation

**Tags:** analytics-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Cross-join asset_inventory with risk_register on asset_id. Returns top 20 assets ordered by risk
score descending.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | str | No | Organisation identifier |

### 2139. `GET` `/api/v1/analytics-engine/query`

**Summary:** Run custom domain query

**Tags:** analytics-engine

**Auth:** See app-level auth (API Key via `X-API-Key` header)

**Description:**

Execute SELECT * from any domain database table with optional WHERE clause. db and table names are
validated against [a-z_]+ to prevent path traversal.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `db` | query | str | Yes | Database name without .db extension (e.g. posture_score) |
| `table` | query | str | Yes | Table name to query |
| `where` | query | Optional | No | Optional SQL WHERE clause |
| `limit` | query | int | No | Maximum rows (1-1000) |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `List`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 2176. `POST` `/api/v1/openclaw/scan`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/openclaw/scan

**Tags:** openclaw

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Start an autonomous pentest of ALDECI itself.  Creates an OpenClaw web_app campaign targeting
ALDECI's own API endpoints, runs MITRE ATT&CK-mapped tasks via the swarm operators, and optionally
fires OWASP Top 10 probes via auto_pentest in the background.  Returns a scan_id that can be polled
via GET /api/v1/openclaw/status.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | str | No | aldeci_self |

**Request Body:** `SelfScanRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `target_url` | str | No | http://localhost:8000 | Base URL of ALDECI to pentest. Defaults to localhost self-test. |
| `campaign_type` | str | No | web_app | OpenClaw campaign type — web_app runs OWASP Top 10 checks. |
| `operators_count` | int | No | 3 |  |
| `run_owasp_checks` | bool | No | True | When True, also runs auto_pentest OWASP Top 10 probes against the target. |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

---

**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error

### 2313. `POST` `/api/v1/health-scorecard/domains`

**Summary:** POST /api/v1/health-scorecard/domains

**Tags:** Security Health Scorecard

**Auth:** API Key required

**Description:**

Upsert a scorecard domain. Status auto-computed from score/max_score ratio.

**Parameters:**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |
|------|----|------|----------|-------|
| `org_id` | query | str | Yes | Organization ID |

**Request Body:** `UpsertDomainRequest`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `domain_name` | str | Yes |  | Domain identifier e.g. 'Vulnerability Management' |
| `domain_category` | str | Yes |  | vulnerability \| compliance \| identity \| network \| endpoint \| cloud \| data \| physical |
| `weight` | float | Yes |  | Domain weight (0-1), clamped automatically |
| `score` | float | Yes |  | Current raw score |
| `max_score` | float | Yes |  | Maximum possible score |

**Responses:**

**200 OK** — `Dict`

**401** — Unauthorized (missing or invalid API key)
**403** — Forbidden (insufficient scope)
**422** — Validation Error (request body/params)
**500** — Internal Server Error
